profile_backing.broad_command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts1 RCH_WORKERS=ts1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-post-p1vbf33-reprofile-8e2db477 cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/(eig_48x48_f64|eigh_48x48_f64|cholesky_1024x1024_f64|qr_1024x1024_f64|pinv_192x192_f64|lu_1024x1024_f64|svd_48x48_f64|svd_48x48_full_f64|matmul_2d_1024x1024x1024_f64)' --sample-size 10 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
profile_backing.focused_lu_command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts1 RCH_WORKERS=ts1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-p1vbf34-lu-baseline cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/lu_1024x1024_f64 --sample-size 80 --measurement-time 5 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
profile_backing.routing_note: The broad profile put Cholesky first, but an 80-sample Cholesky rerun normalized to [52.064, 52.570, 53.084] ms. The 80-sample LU baseline was [71.216, 77.821, 84.661] ms, making LU the focused target.

baseline.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts1 RCH_WORKERS=ts1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-p1vbf34-lu-baseline cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/lu_1024x1024_f64 --sample-size 80 --measurement-time 5 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot

lever.summary: Reuse LU blocked-path L21/U12 panel scratch buffers across panels and fill them with extend_from_slice instead of allocating and zero-filling fresh Vecs before overwriting every element.
lever.alien_graveyard_primitive: Region/arena-style scratch reuse and allocation-churn elimination for hot loops.

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts1 RCH_WORKERS=ts1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-p1vbf35-lu-scratch-after cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/lu_1024x1024_f64 --sample-size 80 --measurement-time 5 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot

isomorphism.ordering: Panel factorization, pivot scan order, U12 solve order, matmul_2d Schur product order, and final A22 subtraction order are unchanged. Only scratch allocation/fill mechanics changed before matmul_2d receives the same row-major L21/U12 values.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: Partial pivoting still uses strict greater-than in ascending row order; the lever does not touch pivot scans or row swaps.
isomorphism.floating_point: No arithmetic expression, accumulation order, FMA use, precision, or NaN/signed-zero operation changed. The same f64 values are copied into the same packed panel slices before the existing matmul_2d call.
isomorphism.rng: No random state is read or written.
isomorphism.errors: Input validation, singular/near-zero behavior, and public output layout are unchanged.

tests.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts1 RCH_WORKERS=ts1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-p1vbf35-lu-tests cargo test -p fj-lax lu_ -- --nocapture
coverage: blocked LU reconstructs and matches scalar on diagonal-dominant matrix
real fast path bit-identical to complex zero-imag path

proof.check: RCH ts1 cargo check -p fj-lax --all-targets passed
proof.clippy: RCH ts1 cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings passed after a separate arithmetic.rs clippy hygiene patch for upstream test warnings
proof.format: git diff --check passed; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on linalg.rs/arithmetic.rs remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated formatting drift outside the committed hunks

related_hygiene.summary: Separate clippy hygiene patch for upstream arithmetic test warnings: introduced a PredicateCase alias and replaced cloned single-element argument slices with std::slice::from_ref.
